title: SatForge
description: A from-scratch CDCL SAT solver studio that grew an SMT brain. The SAT core does two-watched literals, VSIDS, first-UIP learning and restarts, emits machine-checkable DRAT proofs of UNSAT and re-verifies them in-browser with an independent RUP/RAT checker, counts models exactly (#SAT, component caching), extracts minimal unsat cores (MUS), and optimizes — a complete weighted MaxSAT engine (linear SAT-UNSAT and core-guided WPM1 over a Generalized Totalizer) plus encoders for N-Queens, Sudoku, coloring, Hamiltonian cycles, integer factoring (a multiplier circuit run backwards) and the zebra puzzle. NEW: a full DPLL(T) SMT solver built on the very same engine — SAT lifted to first-order theories. A lazy DPLL(T) loop abstracts a formula's Boolean skeleton, and on each candidate hands the theory atoms to from-scratch decision procedures: EUF (equality + uninterpreted functions) by proof-producing congruence closure with curried applications and minimal conflict explanations; linear real & integer arithmetic (QF_LRA/QF_LIA) by a general simplex (Dutertre–de Moura) over EXACT δ-rationals — no floating point anywhere — with conflict explanations and integer branch-and-bound; and mixed QF_UFLIA by Ackermann reduction so uninterpreted functions and arithmetic combine soundly without Nelson-Oppen. Ships a tolerant SMT-LIB 2 parser and an SMT Studio with a curated example library (congruence closure, strict ordering cycles, integer pigeonhole, UF+arithmetic) that shows the verdict, the model, and the exact theory lemmas the solver learned to close the search. NEWEST: a complete QF_BV BIT-VECTOR engine by EAGER BIT-BLASTING onto the very same CDCL core — every fixed-width bit-vector operation lowered to a from-scratch hardware circuit (ripple-carry adder, shift-add multiplier, restoring division, barrel shifters for shl/lshr/ashr, signed div/rem/mod, comparators, concat/extract/extend/rotate), so the SAT verdict IS the SMT verdict: sound and complete, no refinement loop. A width-checking SMT-LIB QF_BV parser (let, indexed operators, #b/#x/(_ bvN m) literals), a model decoder (hex/bin/unsigned/signed) that re-checks every model against an independent BigInt reference, and DRAT-CERTIFIED UNSAT — an unsatisfiable bit-vector encoding is re-verified by the existing independent RUP/RAT checker, so the answer is machine-checked. Examples factor integers by running an 8×8 multiplier backwards, prove the XOR-swap and the overflow-free average bit trick, and split signed from unsigned order. NEWEST: a complete QF_AX THEORY OF ARRAYS — McCarthy select/store, constant arrays, and full EXTENSIONALITY — decided without any new theory solver by REDUCING arrays to the EUF + arithmetic already present: read-over-write purification rewrites every select(store(a,i,v),j) into a fresh element pinned by the McCarthy axioms (recursing through nested writes), surviving reads become an uninterpreted function the congruence-closure engine already handles, and array equality is encoded by a Skolem witness index plus saturated-index agreement clauses (the Stump–Barrett–Dill scheme, made finite and ground), so both existing theories inherit arrays for free and QF_ALIA (arrays + integer arithmetic) just works. The SMT-LIB parser learned (Array I E) sorts, select/store and ((as const ...) v); the Studio renders the array contents the solver committed to as per-array cell tables. NEWEST: a complete QF_DT THEORY OF ALGEBRAIC DATATYPES — constructors, selectors and testers over lists, trees, pairs, enums and Nat — likewise decided with NO new theory solver, by REDUCING datatypes to the EUF + linear-integer arithmetic already present. The reduction is purely additive: it conjoins the free-term-algebra axioms (exactly-one-tester exhaustiveness + disjointness; constructor pinning is_C(C(…)) and sel_i(C(a))=a_i, which yields injectivity for free through selector congruence; the tester link is_C(t) → t = C(sel_1(t),…) instantiated to a bounded selector depth) plus the one genuinely non-EUF ingredient — ACYCLICITY BY INTEGER RANK: a fresh rank:D→Int with rank(t) > rank(child) on every constructor edge, so over a finite set of ground terms a strict > ordering is exactly 'no term is its own subterm' and the existing simplex/branch-and-bound refutes impossible cyclic values like x = cons(a,x). Datatypes thus reduce to QF_UFLIA, Ackermann-combined like UFLIA/QF_ALIA, so QF_DTLIA (datatypes with integer fields) just works. The SMT-LIB parser learned declare-datatype/declare-datatypes (mutually recursive), constructor/selector applications and the ((_ is C) t) tester. Everything is brute-force certified: 3000 random EUF formulas cross-checked against congruence enumeration, 2500 QF_LRA formulas against Fourier–Motzkin, 1200 mixed UF+arithmetic formulas against an Ackermann+FM reference, 1500 random bit-vector formulas against full assignment enumeration with every operator exhaustively checked, ~3700 random array formulas (non-extensional AND extensional) against an independent finite-model enumerator, and ~1500 random datatype formulas against an independent finite-TREE-model enumerator (every value a real constructor tree, testers reading the real root) that shares no code with the reduction. NEWEST: a complete QF_S BOUNDED THEORY OF STRINGS — the hardest classic theory (unbounded string equations are undecidable), added in the same style with NO new theory solver, by REDUCING strings (up to a length bound L) to the EUF + linear-integer arithmetic already present. Each string term becomes a length |s| (the reused str.len symbol) plus L integer code-units str.char$(s,k) — an uninterpreted String×Int→Int function, so EUF gives congruence for free and Ackermann already combines it with the simplex — with padding past the end pinned to a sentinel so a string's value IS its code-units. McCarthy-style per-operator axioms unfold str.++ (concatenation), str.at, str.substr, str.contains/prefixof/suffixof and str.indexof over the ≤L positions into ordinary eq/arith atoms; string equality is kept as an EUF atom AND tied to position-wise code-unit agreement, so '=' means VALUE equality ('ab'·'c' = 'abc'), not term identity. Because the alphabet is constrained only up to equality, the small-model property makes this sound and complete WITHIN the length bound (so x = 'a'·x is refuted by the simplex from |x| = 1 + |x|). The SMT-LIB parser learned the String sort, double-quoted literals and the str.* operators, and the Studio reconstructs each variable's solved text. Everything is brute-force certified: 3000 random EUF formulas cross-checked against congruence enumeration, 2500 QF_LRA formulas against Fourier–Motzkin, 1200 mixed UF+arithmetic formulas against an Ackermann+FM reference, 1500 random bit-vector formulas against full assignment enumeration with every operator exhaustively checked, ~3700 random array formulas (non-extensional AND extensional) against an independent finite-model enumerator, ~1500 random datatype formulas against an independent finite-TREE-model enumerator, and ~900 random bounded-string formulas against an independent concrete-string enumerator (plain JavaScript string semantics, sharing no code with the reduction). NEWEST: CRAIG INTERPOLATION and SAT-BASED UNBOUNDED MODEL CHECKING (McMillan 2003) — SatForge stops reasoning about one formula and starts PROVING WHOLE SYSTEMS SAFE FOR ALL TIME. A from-scratch PROOF-LOGGING CDCL records a resolution refutation (a DAG of partition-tagged input clauses and pivoted resolution steps, down to the empty clause) for every UNSAT, and McMillan's algorithm reads a Craig interpolant straight off it — a formula I with A⟹I, I∧B unsat, over only the vocabulary A and B share. Built on that, an INTERPOLATION-BASED MODEL CHECKER takes a finite-state transition system (Init, Trans, Bad), runs bounded model checking, and iterates the interpolant of one unrolled step into an INDUCTIVE INVARIANT that proves the bad state unreachable on every execution — or returns a concrete shortest counterexample trace. A Model Checker studio ships a gallery (a modulo-6 counter, a saturating counter that overflows, a mutual-exclusion protocol and its broken variant, a traffic-light controller, a token ring), each run live with verdict, invariant or counterexample, the interpolation search trace, AND an INDEPENDENT EXPLICIT-STATE BFS REACHABILITY ORACLE (sharing no code with the SAT/interpolation path) shown beside it; plus an Interpolation panel that computes and exhaustively verifies the interpolant of any editable A/B clause pair. Certified the project's way: the proof solver agrees with brute force AND the main engine on 1500 random CNFs (every model verified), ~500 random UNSAT partitions whose interpolants pass all three Craig properties by exhaustive enumeration, and 300 random total transition systems plus the whole gallery where the model checker must match the BFS oracle on verdict, inductive invariant AND shortest counterexample. A SECOND, INDEPENDENT PROOF RULE — simple-path k-INDUCTION — runs beside interpolation in the studio, so two independent proofs and the explicit-state oracle must all agree. NEWEST: a SOLVER LAB that turns the engine's heuristics into PARAMETERS (restarts, clause deletion, phase saving, VSIDS decay rate, and a genuinely-uniform random branching mode) and RACES the same proved-correct CDCL core against itself across a reproducible benchmark suite — random 3-SAT at the α ≈ 4.26 phase transition, pigeonhole UNSAT, graph coloring and Langford pairings — scoring the field the way the SAT Competition does: a from-scratch SVG CACTUS PLOT, a PAR-2 leaderboard, and a per-instance speed heatmap, all streamed off the main thread by a dedicated worker. It doubles as a SOUNDNESS ORACLE: because every configuration is the same complete solver with a single knob flipped, they must reach the SAME verdict on every instance they decide, so the panel that shows 'restarts make us faster' also continuously re-proves that no heuristic can make us WRONG — cross-checked in the harness where all eleven configurations match exhaustive truth-table brute force on hundreds of random CNFs. NEWEST: a from-scratch IC3 / PDR engine (PROPERTY-DIRECTED REACHABILITY, Bradley 2011) — a THIRD, independent unbounded-safety prover that, unlike interpolation, NEVER UNROLLS THE TRANSITION RELATION. It grows a ladder of over-approximating CNF frames F0=Init ⊆ F1 ⊆ … using only single-step SAT queries, blocks each reachable bad state by a recursively-discharged proof obligation, learns a blocking clause that it INDUCTIVELY GENERALIZES (Bradley's MIC — drop literals while the clause stays inductive relative to the previous frame), pushes clauses forward, and declares safety the moment two adjacent frames coincide (their conjunction is an inductive invariant); unsafety is established by a chain of obligations back to Init, with the shortest concrete counterexample materialized by an honest bounded unrolling. The Model Checker studio now shows FOUR verdicts side by side — interpolation, IC3/PDR, k-induction and the explicit-state BFS oracle, only green when all four agree — plus an IC3 proof panel with a live frame-ladder, MIC/obligation/SAT-query stats, and PDR's own machine-checked inductive invariant (discovered by a completely different route than the interpolation invariant), and a new maximal-length LFSR gallery example whose 'never latches up at zero' safety is proven by the one-clause invariant register≠0. Certified by six new assertions cross-checking PDR's verdicts, invariants and shortest counterexamples against BFS, interpolation AND k-induction on 220 random systems plus the gallery. Part of 296 self-tests in all.
